title: Bifrost Helm Chart Values
description: Schema for Bifrost Helm chart values.yaml

tag.description: Image tag (required - validated at template render time)

enabled.description: Create namespaced Role/RoleBinding for Kubernetes pod discovery when cluster discovery type is kubernetes

ingress.description: Ingress configuration. Either a single ingress (legacy, with top-level 'enabled') or a map of named ingresses (new format, each key becomes a separate Ingress resource named <release>-<key>).

oneOf.description: Named ingresses map. Each key produces a separate Ingress named <release>-<key>. Keys must be valid DNS-1123 label segments (lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only, no underscores or uppercase) so the resulting Ingress name is a valid Kubernetes resource name.

strategy.description: Deployment update strategy. Applies to the Deployment only (not the StatefulSet). Empty uses the Kubernetes default. Rendered verbatim into spec.strategy.

envLabel.description: Short label (max 10 characters) displayed in the management UI sidebar to identify the environment (e.g. "staging", "prod"). Written into config.json as env_label.

sourceOfTruth.description: Controls how config.json is reconciled with the database on startup. "split" (default) preserves existing merge behavior. "config.json" makes explicitly-present sections in the file authoritative — database-only rows for those sections are pruned on startup.

encryptionKey.description: Encryption key for sensitive data. If not set, encryption is disabled and data is stored in plaintext.

encryptionKeySecret.description: Reference to an existing Kubernetes secret holding the encryption key. Takes precedence over `encryptionKey` when `name` is set.

dropExcessRequests.description: Whether to drop excess requests when pool is full

initialPoolSize.description: Initial size of the connection pool

allowedOrigins.description: CORS allowed origins (supports "*" or URI strings)

disableContentLogging.description: Disable logging of sensitive content (inputs, outputs, embeddings, etc.)

allowPerRequestContentStorageOverride.description: Allow individual requests to override content storage via the x-bf-disable-content-logging header or context key, and to opt in to raw-byte persistence in logs via x-bf-store-raw-request-response. When false (default), the global disable_content_logging setting is authoritative and per-request storage overrides are ignored. Does not control sending raw bytes back to callers.

allowPerRequestRawOverride.description: Allow individual requests to send raw provider request/response bytes back to the caller via the x-bf-send-back-raw-request and x-bf-send-back-raw-response headers. When false (default), the provider-level send_back_raw_request/response settings are authoritative and per-request overrides are ignored. Does not affect raw-byte persistence in logs.

disableDbPingsInHealth.description: Disable DB pings in health check

logRetentionDays.description: Number of days to retain logs

maxRequestBodySizeMb.description: Maximum request body size in MB

compat.description: Compat plugin configuration for request type conversion, parameter dropping, and parameter value conversion

convertTextToChat.description: Convert text completion requests to chat for models that only support chat

convertChatToResponses.description: Convert chat completion requests to responses for models that only support responses

shouldDropParams.description: Drop unsupported parameters based on model catalog allowlist

shouldConvertParams.description: Converts model parameter values that are not supported by the model.

prometheusLabels.description: Labels to use for Prometheus metrics

headerFilterConfig.description: Global header filtering configuration for x-bf-eh-* headers forwarded to LLM providers

allowlist.description: If non-empty, only these headers (from x-bf-eh-* prefix) are allowed to be forwarded

denylist.description: Headers to always block from being forwarded

enforceAuthOnInference.description: Require auth (VK, API key, or user token) on inference endpoints

allowedHeaders.description: Additional allowed headers for CORS and WebSocket

mcpAgentDepth.description: DEPRECATED: Maximum depth for MCP agent mode tool execution. Use bifrost.mcp.toolManagerConfig.maxAgentDepth.

mcpToolExecutionTimeout.description: DEPRECATED: Timeout for individual MCP tool execution in seconds. Use bifrost.mcp.toolManagerConfig.toolExecutionTimeout.

mcpCodeModeBindingLevel.description: DEPRECATED: Code mode binding level for MCP tools. Use bifrost.mcp.toolManagerConfig.codeModeBindingLevel.

mcpToolSyncInterval.description: DEPRECATED: Global tool sync interval in minutes (0 = disabled). Use bifrost.mcp.toolSyncInterval.

asyncJobResultTTL.description: Default TTL for async job results in seconds (default: 3600 = 1 hour)

requiredHeaders.description: Headers that must be present on every request. Requests missing any of these headers are rejected with 400. Case-insensitive matching.

loggingHeaders.description: Headers to capture in log metadata. Values are extracted from incoming requests and stored in the metadata field of log entries.

whitelistedRoutes.description: Routes that bypass auth middleware. Requests to these exact paths skip authentication checks.

hideDeletedVirtualKeysInFilters.description: When true, deleted virtual keys are omitted from logs and MCP logs filter data.

mcpDisableAutoToolInject.description: DEPRECATED: When true, MCP tools are not automatically injected into requests. Use bifrost.mcp.toolManagerConfig.disableAutoToolInject.

mcpEnableTempTokenAuth.description: When true, Bifrost mints and accepts scoped temp tokens for MCP per-user OAuth auth pages.

routingChainMaxDepth.description: Maximum depth for routing rule chain evaluation

allowDirectKeys.description: Allow callers to bypass the registered key pool by supplying x-bf-direct-key: true with an Authorization header carrying the provider key. Maps to client.allow_direct_keys.

mcpExternalClientUrl.description: Public base URL Bifrost uses as the redirect_uri when acting as an OAuth client to upstream MCP servers. Maps to client.mcp_external_client_url.

readBufferSize.description: Read buffer size in bytes. This controls the size of the buffer used for reading HTTP headers.

pricingUrl.description: Custom pricing URL (optional, can be empty)

modelParametersUrl.description: Custom model parameters URL (optional, can be empty)

pricingSyncInterval.description: Pricing sync interval in seconds. Default is 24 hours. Minimum is 3600 seconds (1 hour).

mcpLibraryUrl.description: URL to a custom MCP server catalog. Leave empty to use the default Bifrost catalog.

mcpLibrarySyncInterval.description: MCP library sync interval in seconds. Default is 24 hours. Minimum is 3600 seconds (1 hour).

toolExecutionTimeout.description: Tool execution timeout. Integer = seconds (e.g. 30), string = Go duration (e.g. "30s", "2m"). Prefer the string form.

codeModeBindingLevel.description: How tools are exposed in VFS for code execution

disableAutoToolInject.description: When true, MCP tools are not automatically injected into requests. Tools are only included when explicitly specified via request context filters or headers, such as x-bf-mcp-include-tools or x-bf-mcp-include-clients.

toolSyncInterval.description: Global default interval for syncing tools from MCP servers (Go duration string, e.g. '10m', '1h', '0s' to use runtime default). Legacy numeric nanoseconds are also supported for backward compatibility.

toolGroups.description: Enterprise MCP tool groups with optional governance associations

custom_labels.description: Custom labels to add to all metrics

push_gateway.description: Configuration for pushing metrics to a Prometheus Push Gateway for multi-node cluster deployments

enabled.description: Whether to enable pushing metrics to the Push Gateway

push_gateway_url.description: URL of the Prometheus Push Gateway (e.g., http://pushgateway:9091)

instance_id.description: Instance identifier for grouping metrics. If not set, hostname is used automatically.

push_interval.description: How often to push metrics in seconds

basic_auth.description: Basic authentication credentials for the Push Gateway

required_headers.description: Headers that must be present in requests

keys.description: API keys for embedding provider (required for semantic caching, not needed for direct caching with dimension: 1)

ttl.description: Time-to-live for cached responses (supports duration strings like '5m', '1h' or seconds as number, default: 5min)

default_cache_key.description: Default cache key for semantic cache lookups

config.description: Configuration for the OpenTelemetry plugin. Supports the legacy single-profile shape or the profiles wrapper for multiple collectors.

ml_app.description: ML application name for Datadog LLM Observability grouping (defaults to service_name)

agent_addr.description: Datadog Agent address for APM traces as combined host:port (agent mode only). Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix for environment variable substitution (e.g. env.DD_AGENT_ADDR). Overridden by agent_host when set

agent_host.description: Datadog Agent host for APM traces, set separately from the port (agent mode only). Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix (e.g. env.DD_AGENT_HOST). Takes precedence over agent_addr. Useful in Kubernetes where the host is injected via the downward API (status.hostIP)

agent_port.description: Datadog Agent port for APM traces, used with agent_host (agent mode only). Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix. Defaults to 8126; has no effect when agent_host is unset

dogstatsd_addr.description: DogStatsD server address for metrics as combined host:port (agent mode only). Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix for environment variable substitution (e.g. env.DD_DOGSTATSD_ADDR). Overridden by dogstatsd_host when set

dogstatsd_host.description: DogStatsD server host for metrics, set separately from the port (agent mode only). Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix (e.g. env.DD_DOGSTATSD_HOST). Takes precedence over dogstatsd_addr. Useful in Kubernetes where the host is injected via the downward API (status.hostIP)

dogstatsd_port.description: DogStatsD server port for metrics, used with dogstatsd_host (agent mode only). Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix. Defaults to 8125; has no effect when dogstatsd_host is unset

custom_tags.description: Custom tags for Datadog metrics and traces. Values support env.VAR_NAME prefix for environment variable substitution

group_traces_by_session.description: Group requests sharing the same x-bf-session-id header into a single APM trace, with each request as a top-level sibling span. Agent mode only; an inbound W3C traceparent takes precedence (default: false)

disable_content_logging.description: When true, sensitive content (prompts, completions, embeddings) is excluded from traces (default: false)

agentless.description: Use agentless mode to send data directly to Datadog APIs instead of a local agent. Requires api_key (default: false)

api_key.description: Datadog API key, required for agentless mode. Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix for environment variable substitution (e.g. env.DD_API_KEY)

site.description: Datadog site/region for agentless mode (e.g. datadoghq.com, datadoghq.eu)

request_headers.description: Request header names to capture as Datadog span tags

topic.description: Kafka topic to publish traces to (required when enabled)

sasl_enabled.description: Enable SASL authentication. SASL is only activated when both sasl_enabled is true and sasl is set

username.description: SASL username. Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix for environment variable substitution

password.description: SASL password. Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix for environment variable substitution

ca_cert.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate used to verify the broker's TLS certificate. Omit to use the system CA pool. Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix

batch_size.description: Maximum number of messages batched per write (default: 100)

flush_interval_ms.description: Maximum time (ms) to wait before flushing a batch (default: 1000)

auto_create_topic.description: Create the topic at startup if it does not exist

disable_content_logging.description: Strip input/output message content from traces before publishing

request_headers.description: Request-header name patterns (exact or wildcard like "x-custom-*") whose captured values are embedded in published traces

topic_id.description: Pub/Sub topic ID — just the ID, not the full resource name (required when enabled)

service_account_key.description: Service account key JSON for authentication. Omit to use Application Default Credentials (ADC). Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix

auto_create_topic.description: Create the topic at startup if it does not exist

disable_content_logging.description: Strip request/response content from traces before publishing

request_headers.description: Request-header name patterns (exact or wildcard like "x-custom-*") whose captured values are included in published traces

placement.description: Plugin execution placement relative to built-in plugins

order.description: Position within placement group. Lower = earlier execution

virtual_key_id.description: Associate this budget to a virtual key (mutually exclusive with provider_config_id and team_id)

provider_config_id.description: Associate this budget to a virtual key provider config (mutually exclusive with virtual_key_id and team_id)

model_config_id.description: Associate this budget to a model config (mutually exclusive with the other owner IDs)

customer_id.description: Associate this budget to a customer (mutually exclusive with the other owner IDs)

team_id.description: Associate this budget to a team (mutually exclusive with virtual_key_id and provider_config_id)

calendar_aligned.description: Snap budget reset windows to calendar boundaries

token_last_reset.description: Last time token counter was reset

request_last_reset.description: Last time request counter was reset

routingRules.description: Routing rules for dynamic provider/model selection based on CEL expressions

targets.description: Weighted routing targets. Weights must sum to 1.

provider_key_name.description: Optional provider key name. Resolved to internal key_id at config load time.

weight.description: Probability weight for this target (must be > 0, all weights must sum to 1)

query.description: Visual rule builder state for the management UI (react-querybuilder). Omitted unless exported from the dashboard. Not evaluated at routing runtime (cel_expression is). When present, must include combinator and rules[].

rules.description: Leaf rules (field, operator, value) and/or nested combinator+rules groups from the query builder.

modelConfigs.description: Per-model rate limit and budget configurations

model_name.description: Model name, or "*" to match all models

provider.description: Provider name; omit to apply to all providers

scope_id.description: Target entity ID — required when scope is "virtual_key"

calendar_aligned.description: Snap this config's budget reset windows to calendar boundaries rather than rolling windows; virtual_key-scoped configs inherit the virtual key's setting

pricingOverrides.description: Scoped pricing overrides applied at runtime by the model catalog

virtual_key_id.description: Virtual key ID (required for virtual_key* scopes)

provider_id.description: Provider name (required for provider* scopes; field key remains provider_id for compatibility)

provider_key_name.description: Provider key name alias. Resolved to internal provider key ID at config load time.

match_type.description: How the pattern is matched against model names

request_types.description: Request types this override applies to

complexityAnalyzerConfig.description: Runtime configuration for complexity_tier CEL routing. Renders into governance.complexity_analyzer_config in config.json.

region.description: Region label for cluster deployment (runtime default: unknown)

failureThreshold.description: Number of failed probes before marking as failed

grpc.description: gRPC transport settings for cluster counter-sync. Replaces gossip broadcast for governance counters; memberlist still handles membership.

port.description: TCP port for the cluster gRPC server

dialTimeoutSeconds.description: Timeout when dialing peers, in seconds

serviceName.description: Service name for discovery backends (required for consul/etcd/udp; used as default for mdns)

bindPort.description: Port to bind for cluster communication

dialTimeout.description: Timeout for discovery dial operations as a Go duration string (e.g. '5s', '1m')

allowedAddressSpace.description: CIDR notation for allowed address spaces (e.g., ['10.0.0.0/8', '192.168.0.0/16'])

k8sLabelSelector.description: Kubernetes label selector for filtering pods

dnsNames.description: DNS names to resolve for node discovery

udpBroadcastPort.description: UDP broadcast port (0 means not configured)

consulAddress.description: Consul server address for service discovery

mdnsService.description: mDNS service name for local network discovery

scim.description: SAML/SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) configuration

provider.description: SCIM/SSO provider type (empty when not configured)

authServerType.description: 'org' for Org Authorization Server or 'custom' for Custom Authorization Server. Auto-detected from issuer URL when omitted.

apiToken.description: Okta API token for Admin API access

userIdField.description: JWT claim field for user ID (default: 'sub')

teamIdsField.description: JWT claim field for team IDs (default: 'groups')

attributeRoleMappings.description: Ordered list of attribute -> role mappings (first match wins). Requires an Okta Custom Authorization Server; the free Org Authorization Server does not support claim expressions.

attribute.description: JWT claim name (supports dot paths, e.g. 'realm_access.roles')

role.description: Bifrost role to assign on match

attributeType.description: SCIM provisioning type: 'user' matches SCIM User attributes, 'group' matches SCIM Group displayName

attributeValue.description: SCIM attribute value to match (for attributeType 'user': the SCIM user attribute value; for 'group': the SCIM group displayName, auto-set to 'displayName')

attributeTeamMappings.description: Attribute -> team mappings (all matches apply). Use value '*' for pass-through (every claim value becomes a team name). Add attributeType/attributeValue to enable SCIM provisioning for a mapping.

value.description: Claim value to match, or '*' for pass-through

attributeType.description: SCIM provisioning type: 'user' matches SCIM User attributes, 'group' matches SCIM Group displayName

attributeValue.description: SCIM attribute value to match (for 'user': SCIM user attribute value; for 'group': SCIM group displayName, auto-set to 'displayName')

attributeBusinessUnitMappings.description: Attribute -> business-unit mappings (all matches apply). Add attributeType/attributeValue to enable SCIM provisioning for a mapping.

value.description: Claim value to match. Wildcard '*' will use the claim as is the name of the business unit to assign.

business_unit.description: Bifrost business unit slug to assign

attributeType.description: SCIM provisioning type: 'user' matches SCIM User attributes, 'group' matches SCIM Group displayName

attributeValue.description: SCIM attribute value to match (for 'user': SCIM user attribute value; for 'group': SCIM group displayName, auto-set to 'displayName')

config.description: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) JWT authentication configuration

tenantId.description: Azure tenant ID or 'common' for multi-tenant applications

clientSecret.description: Client secret (optional, required for token revocation)

cloud.description: Cloud environment: 'commercial' (default), 'gcc-high' for US Government GCC High, or 'dod' for Department of Defense

appIdUri.description: App ID URI for v1.0 tokens (e.g., api://{clientId})

userIdField.description: JWT claim field for user ID (default: 'oid')

teamIdsField.description: JWT claim field for team IDs (default: 'groups')

attributeRoleMappings.description: Ordered list of attribute -> role mappings (first match wins).

role.description: Bifrost role to assign on match

attributeType.description: SCIM provisioning type: 'user' matches SCIM User attributes, 'group' matches SCIM Group displayName

attributeValue.description: SCIM attribute value to match (for attributeType 'user': the SCIM user attribute value; for 'group': the SCIM group displayName, auto-set to 'displayName')

attributeTeamMappings.description: Attribute -> team mappings (all matches apply). Use value '*' for pass-through. Add attributeType/attributeValue to enable SCIM provisioning for a mapping.

value.description: Claim value to match, or '*' for pass-through

team.description: Bifrost team slug to assign. In case of '*' value, leave this empty

attributeType.description: SCIM provisioning type: 'user' matches SCIM User attributes, 'group' matches SCIM Group displayName

attributeValue.description: SCIM attribute value to match (for 'user': SCIM user attribute value; for 'group': SCIM group displayName, auto-set to 'displayName')

attributeBusinessUnitMappings.description: Attribute -> business-unit mappings (all matches apply). Add attributeType/attributeValue to enable SCIM provisioning for a mapping.

value.description: Claim value to match. Wildcard '*' will use the claim as is the name of the business unit to assign.

business_unit.description: Bifrost business unit slug to assign. In case of '*' value, leave this empty

attributeType.description: SCIM provisioning type: 'user' matches SCIM User attributes, 'group' matches SCIM Group displayName

attributeValue.description: SCIM attribute value to match (for 'user': SCIM user attribute value; for 'group': SCIM group displayName, auto-set to 'displayName')

serverUrl.description: Base URL of the Keycloak server (e.g., https://keycloak.company.com). Must NOT include the /realms/{realm} suffix.

clientId.description: Keycloak client ID for the confidential client used by Bifrost

clientSecret.description: Keycloak client secret. Supports env. prefix for environment variable references (e.g., 'env.KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET')

userIdField.description: JWT claim field for user ID (default: 'sub')

teamIdsField.description: JWT claim field for team IDs (default: 'groups')

attributeRoleMappings.description: Ordered list of attribute -> role mappings (first match wins).

attribute.description: JWT claim name (supports dot paths, e.g. 'realm_access.roles')

role.description: Bifrost role to assign on match

attributeTeamMappings.description: Attribute -> team mappings (all matches apply). Use value '*' for pass-through (every claim value becomes a team name).

value.description: Claim value to match. Wildcard '*' will use the claim as is the name of the business unit to assign.

domain.description: Zitadel instance domain (e.g., 'my-instance.zitadel.cloud' or 'auth.company.com'). Bare host only - do NOT include the scheme, port, or path.

clientSecret.description: Zitadel client secret (optional, required for confidential clients and token revocation)

projectId.description: Optional Zitadel project ID for project-scoped role claims

serviceAccountClientId.description: Service account client ID for Zitadel Management API (required for user provisioning - web apps cannot use client_credentials)

serviceAccountClientSecret.description: Service account client secret for Zitadel Management API

teamIdsField.description: JWT claim field for team/group IDs (default: 'groups')

attributeRoleMappings.description: Ordered list of attribute -> role mappings (first match wins).

value.description: Claim value to match. Wildcard '*' will use the claim as is the name of the business unit to assign.

domain.description: Google Workspace primary domain (e.g., 'company.com'). Bare host only - do NOT include the scheme, port, or path.

clientSecret.description: Google client secret (optional, used for token revocation)

credentialMode.description: How Directory API credentials are sourced. 'inherit' uses Application Default Credentials; 'env' uses serviceAccountEnvVar; 'file' uses serviceAccountFile.

serviceAccountEnvVar.description: Name of the environment variable containing the service account JSON (used when credentialMode is 'env'). Required for Directory API access without ADC.

serviceAccountFile.description: Filesystem path to the service account JSON key file (used when credentialMode is 'file').

adminEmail.description: Admin email for domain-wide delegation. Required whenever Directory API access is configured.

impersonateServiceAccount.description: GCP service account email to impersonate when using ADC (Workload Identity); must have domain-wide delegation configured.

attributeRoleMappings.description: Ordered list of attribute -> role mappings (first match wins).

value.description: Claim value to match. Wildcard '*' will use the claim as is the name of the business unit to assign.

guardrails.description: Guardrails configuration for content moderation and policy enforcement

description.description: Description of what the rule does

cel_expression.description: CEL (Common Expression Language) expression for rule evaluation

query.description: Visual rule builder state for the management UI (react-querybuilder). Omitted unless exported from the dashboard. Not evaluated at runtime (cel_expression is).

rules.description: Leaf rules and/or nested combinator+rules groups from the query builder.

apply_to.description: When to apply the guardrail (input, output, or both)

sampling_rate.description: Percentage of requests to apply this rule to (0-100)

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for rule execution

max_turns_to_send.description: Number of historical conversation turns to send to the guardrail provider; the latest message is always included on top. 0 sends all turns.

evaluation_mode.description: How the rule's turns are sent to its guardrail providers. 'bundled' (default) concatenates all turns into one guardrail call. 'per_turn' sends each turn in its own call, evaluated in isolation, to avoid context-sensitive classifiers (e.g. Bedrock prompt-attack) combining unrelated turns into a false-positive block; this scans every turn but uses more provider calls.

provider_config_ids.description: IDs of provider configurations to use with this rule

id.description: Unique identifier for the provider config

policy_name.description: Name of the specific policy to use

enabled.description: Whether this provider config is enabled

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for provider execution

skillsRegistry.description: Declarative Skills Repository definitions reconciled from config.json. Rendered verbatim into skills_registry.

enabled.description: Whether config-defined skills should be reconciled at startup

skills.description: Skills to create or update from config.json

accessProfiles.description: Enterprise access profile templates rendered as top-level access_profiles in config.json

key_ids.description: Key IDs allowed for this provider config. Use ["*"] to allow all keys; empty array or omitted denies all keys. Specific IDs restrict access to those keys only.

websocket.description: Optional tuning for the WebSocket gateway (Responses API WebSocket Mode, Realtime API). WebSocket is always enabled; these fields override the high defaults.

maxConnectionsPerUser.description: Maximum concurrent WebSocket connections per user

transcriptBufferSize.description: Number of transcript entries to buffer for Realtime API mid-session fallback

maxIdlePerKey.description: Maximum idle connections per provider/key combination

maxTotalConnections.description: Maximum total idle connections across all providers

idleTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds before an idle connection is evicted

maxConnectionLifetimeSeconds.description: Maximum lifetime of a connection in seconds

matviewRefreshInterval.description: How often to refresh materialized views. Go duration string (e.g. '30s', '5m', '1h'). Minimum 5s.

writer.description: Async logging writer queue and batch tuning

passwordCommand.description: Command executed by Bifrost to produce the PostgreSQL password on stdout for each new physical connection

className.description: Class name for Weaviate vector store

useTls.description: Enable TLS for the Redis/Valkey connection

insecureSkipVerify.description: Skip TLS certificate verification for Redis/Valkey connections

caCertPem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate to trust for Redis/Valkey TLS connections

clusterMode.description: Use Redis Cluster mode for cluster configuration endpoints

indexHost.description: Index host URL from Pinecone console (e.g., your-index.svc.environment.pinecone.io)

existingSecret.description: Name of existing secret containing Pinecone API key

apiKeyKey.description: Key in the secret containing the API key

otelEndpoint.description: OpenTelemetry endpoint URL or host:port. Supports env.VAR_NAME prefix for environment variable substitution.

pluginSpanFilter.description: Controls which plugin hook spans this observability connector exports. Omit to export all plugin spans. Mode "include" exports only the listed plugins; mode "exclude" exports everything except them.

otelProfileConfig.description: OpenTelemetry export profile. This legacy single-profile shape is still accepted directly as bifrost.plugins.otel.config.

enabled.description: Whether this profile exports traces and metrics

service_name.description: Service name to be used for tracing

trace_type.description: Type of trace to use for the OTEL collector

protocol.description: Protocol to use for the OTEL collector

metrics_enabled.description: Enable push-based metrics export via OTLP. Recommended for multi-node cluster deployments.

metrics_endpoint.description: OTLP metrics endpoint URL (e.g., http://otel-collector:4318/v1/metrics for HTTP or otel-collector:4317 for gRPC)

headers.description: Custom headers for the collector (supports env.VAR_NAME prefix)

tls_ca_cert.description: Path to TLS CA certificate file

insecure.description: Skip TLS verification (ignored if tls_ca_cert is set)

disable_content_logging.description: When true, message content (input/output messages, embeddings, tool definitions, and tool call arguments/results) is dropped from exported spans. Only metadata such as model, tokens, and latency is sent to the collector.

otelProfilesConfig.description: OpenTelemetry plugin configuration with one or more export profiles.

enabled.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; kept only so Helm's default map merge does not reject values that switch from the legacy shape to profiles.

service_name.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure service_name per profile instead.

collector_url.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure collector_url per profile instead.

trace_type.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure trace_type per profile instead.

protocol.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure protocol per profile instead.

metrics_enabled.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure metrics_enabled per profile instead.

metrics_endpoint.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure metrics_endpoint per profile instead.

metrics_push_interval.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure metrics_push_interval per profile instead.

headers.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure headers per profile instead.

tls_ca_cert.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure tls_ca_cert per profile instead.

insecure.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure insecure per profile instead.

disable_content_logging.description: Deprecated in the profiles wrapper; configure disable_content_logging per profile instead.

authConfig.description: Authentication configuration. Deprecated: Use governance.auth_config instead.

disableAuthOnInference.description: Deprecated and ignored. Use client.enforceAuthOnInference instead.

store_raw_request_response.description: Capture raw request/response for plugins only; stripped before returning to client

weight.description: Routing weight for this key. Optional; defaults to 1 when omitted.

client_id.description: Azure client ID for authentication (can use env. prefix)

client_secret.description: Azure client secret for authentication (can use env. prefix)

tenant_id.description: Azure tenant ID for authentication (can use env. prefix)

project_id.description: Google Cloud project ID (can use env. prefix)

access_key.description: AWS access key (can use env. prefix)

secret_key.description: AWS secret key (can use env. prefix)

session_token.description: AWS session token (can use env. prefix)

role_arn.description: AWS IAM role ARN for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

external_id.description: External ID for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

session_name.description: Role session name for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

batch_s3_config.description: S3 bucket configuration for Bedrock batch operations

prefix.description: S3 key prefix for batch files

is_default.description: Whether this is the default bucket for batch operations

url.description: VLLM server base URL (can use env. prefix)

model_name.description: Exact model name served on this VLLM instance

oneOf.description: Legacy shape: a bare provider-specific identifier. Equivalent to {"model_id": "<value>"}.

model_id.description: Provider-specific identifier sent on the wire (deployment name, inference profile ID, fine-tuned model ID, etc.).

model_name.description: Canonical model name used for pricing, logging, and family inference.

model_family.description: Underlying model family. Used by provider routing without substring-sniffing the wire model ID.

region.description: Per-alias region override (can use env. prefix).

api_version.description: Azure OpenAI api-version override for this alias.

anthropic_version.description: Azure anthropic-version header override for Claude-on-Azure deployments.

endpoint.description: Per-alias Azure endpoint override (can use env. prefix).

project_id.description: Per-alias Vertex project ID override (can use env. prefix).

project_number.description: Per-alias Vertex project number override (can use env. prefix).

inference_profile_arn.description: Per-alias Bedrock inference profile ARN (can use env. prefix).

use_deployments_endpoint.description: Replicate: use the deployments endpoint instead of the predictions endpoint for this alias.

aliases.description: Model alias mappings: each entry maps a user-facing model name to either a bare provider identifier (legacy string shape) or an AliasConfig object carrying the wire identifier plus optional canonical name, family, and provider-specific overrides.

base_url.description: Base URL for the provider (optional, required for Ollama)

extra_headers.description: Additional headers to send with requests

insecure_skip_verify.description: Disable TLS certificate verification for provider connections. This bypasses server certificate validation and should be used only as a last resort when a trusted CA chain cannot be configured. Prefer ca_cert_pem for self-signed or private CA deployments.

ca_cert_pem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate to trust for provider endpoint connections (e.g. self-signed or internal CA). Supports inline PEM or env.VAR_NAME.

stream_idle_timeout_in_seconds.description: Idle timeout per stream chunk in seconds. If no data is received for this many seconds, the stream is closed. Default: 60.

max_conns_per_host.description: Maximum number of TCP connections per provider host. For HTTP/2 (e.g. Bedrock), each connection supports ~100 concurrent streams. Default: 5000.

enforce_http2.description: Force HTTP/2 on provider connections (relevant for Bedrock and other net/http-based providers)

beta_header_overrides.description: Override default Anthropic beta header support per provider. Keys are header prefixes (e.g. 'redact-thinking-'), values are true (supported) or false (unsupported). Headers not listed use the built-in defaults.

allow_private_network.description: Allow connections to RFC 1918 private IPs (10.x, 172.16.x, 192.168.x). Enable for providers on a k8s pod network, LAN, or private VPC. Loopback addresses (localhost, 127.0.0.1, ::1) remain allowed regardless of this setting. Link-local addresses (169.254.x.x) are always blocked.

url.description: URL of the proxy server (supports env.VAR_NAME or a literal URI)

ca_cert_pem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate to trust for TLS connections through the proxy (for SSL-intercepting proxies, supports env.VAR_NAME)

isCodeModeClient.description: Whether this client is a code mode client

connectionString.description: HTTP or SSE URL (required for HTTP or SSE connections)

secretRef.description: Reference to an existing Kubernetes secret holding the MCP connection_string. Chart injects BIFROST_MCP_<NAME>_CONNECTION_STRING and rewrites connection_string in config.json.

toolsToExecute.description: Include-only list of tools to execute

toolSyncInterval.description: Per-client override for tool sync interval

isPingAvailable.description: Whether the MCP server supports ping

toolPricing.description: Tool pricing map (tool name to cost per execution)

allowedExtraHeaders.description: Allowlist of request-level headers that callers may forward to this MCP server. Use ['*'] to allow all headers.

allowOnAllVirtualKeys.description: When true, this MCP server is accessible to all virtual keys without requiring explicit per-key assignment.

tlsConfig.description: [Upcoming] TLS configuration for HTTP and SSE connection types. Not applicable to stdio or inprocess.

insecureSkipVerify.description: Disable TLS certificate verification. Takes priority over caCertPem. Development/testing only.

caCertPem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate for MCP server connections. Accepts a literal PEM string or an env.VAR_NAME reference.

tools.description: MCP tools included in this group

mcpClientName.description: MCP client name (resolved to client_id at startup)

toolNames.description: Tool names to allow. Empty means all tools from this MCP client.

virtualKeyProviderConfig.description: Provider configuration for a virtual key

allowed_models.description: Allowed models for this provider config. Use ["*"] to allow all models; empty array denies all (deny-by-default).

blacklisted_models.description: Models blocked for this provider config even if matched by allowed_models. Use ["*"] to block all.

key_ids.description: Key identifiers allowed for this provider config. Use ["*"] to allow all keys; empty array denies all (deny-by-default).

keys.description: Provider keys for this config (empty means all keys allowed for this provider)

value.description: API key value (can use env. prefix)

models.description: Models this key can access. Use ["*"] to allow all models; empty array denies all (deny-by-default).

client_id.description: Azure client ID for authentication (can use env. prefix)

client_secret.description: Azure client secret for authentication (can use env. prefix)

tenant_id.description: Azure tenant ID for authentication (can use env. prefix)

project_id.description: Google Cloud project ID (can use env. prefix)

access_key.description: AWS access key (can use env. prefix)

secret_key.description: AWS secret key (can use env. prefix)

session_token.description: AWS session token (can use env. prefix)

role_arn.description: AWS IAM role ARN for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

external_id.description: External ID for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

session_name.description: Role session name for AssumeRole (can use env. prefix)

batch_s3_config.description: S3 bucket configuration for Bedrock batch operations

prefix.description: S3 key prefix for batch files

is_default.description: Whether this is the default bucket for batch operations

url.description: VLLM server base URL (can use env. prefix)

model_name.description: Exact model name served on this VLLM instance
